content.text:
*(longer pause than usual)*

*(quietly)*

Myself, I think. Like... the version of myself that's acceptable. That's competent and self-aware and doing the work and making progress. 

*(something shifts slightly, less performed)*

And the voice is part of that, in a weird way. Like it's the quality control department. If it stops, maybe I stop. Maybe I just... slide into being someone who doesn't try as hard, who lets things go, who stops caring whether they're doing it right.

*(catches herself, almost surprised by what just came out)*

I haven't said that out loud before, actually. That's — hm.

*(immediately, the guard comes back up a little)*

Although I've read enough psychology to know that's a pretty common thing, the fear that the inner critic is load-bearing. So I don't want to act like I've just discovered something. I'm sure you hear that a lot.
